title.en: The Big Picture: BSS, STAs, and the Three-Step Connection
title.zh: 全景图：BSS、STA 与连接的三个步骤

blocks.en: Before a phone can stream a video over Wi-Fi, it has to do something we rarely think about: it has to join the network. To understand how, you only need three actors. A station (STA) is any Wi-Fi device — your phone, laptop, or watch. An access point (AP) is the special station that bridges the air to the wider network. And the basic service set (BSS) is the group: the AP plus all the stations currently talking to it.
blocks.zh: 手机在用 Wi-Fi 播放视频之前，要先做一件我们平时不太留意的事：加入网络。要理解这个过程，只需认识三个角色。站点（STA）是任意一台 Wi-Fi 设备——你的手机、笔记本或手表。接入点（AP）是一种特殊的站点，它把空口与更大的网络连接起来。而基本服务集（BSS）就是这个群体：AP 加上当前正与它通信的所有站点。

blocks.en: The BSS is the basic building block of a Wi-Fi LAN. The most common kind — the one your home router runs — is an infrastructure BSS, where every station talks through the AP rather than directly to its peers.
blocks.zh: BSS 是 Wi-Fi 局域网的基本构建单元。最常见的一种——也就是你家路由器运行的那种——是基础设施 BSS（infrastructure BSS）：每个站点都通过 AP 通信，而不是与其他站点直接对话。

blocks.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 320 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><ellipse cx='160' cy='90' rx='140' ry='75' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-dasharray='6 5'/><rect x='135' y='30' width='50' height='30' rx='4' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'/><text x='160' y='50' fill='currentColor' font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'>AP</text><line x1='160' y1='62' x2='160' y2='75' stroke='currentColor'/><path d='M150 24 q10 -10 20 0' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'/><path d='M145 20 q15 -16 30 0' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'/><rect x='40' y='120' width='44' height='28' rx='4' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'/><text x='62' y='138' fill='currentColor' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>STA</text><rect x='238' y='120' width='44' height='28' rx='4' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'/><text x='260' y='138' fill='currentColor' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>STA</text><line x1='148' y1='55' x2='72' y2='120' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='3 4'/><line x1='172' y1='55' x2='250' y2='120' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='3 4'/></svg>

caption.en: An infrastructure BSS: one AP and the stations associated with it, inside its coverage area (the basic service area).
caption.zh: 一个基础设施 BSS：一个 AP 和与之关联的站点，都处于它的覆盖范围（基本服务区）之内。

blocks.en: Membership in a BSS is dynamic — stations switch on and off, walk into range and out again. So joining is not a single magic moment; it is a sequence a station works through to earn the right to send real data. The standard frames it as three steps: discovery, then authentication, then association.
blocks.zh: BSS 的成员关系是动态的——站点会开关机，会走进或走出覆盖范围。因此“加入”并不是某个一瞬间完成的魔法，而是站点为换取发送真实数据的资格而一步步走完的流程。标准把它划分为三个步骤：先发现，再认证，最后关联。

blocks.en:
sequenceDiagram
    participant S as STA (phone)
    participant A as AP
    Note over S,A: 1. Discovery
    A-->>S: Beacon (passive scan)
    S->>A: Probe Request (active scan)
    A-->>S: Probe Response
    Note over S,A: 2. Authentication
    S->>A: Authentication
    A-->>S: Authentication (success)
    Note over S,A: 3. Association
    S->>A: Association Request
    A-->>S: Association Response (success)
    Note over S,A: Now data frames can flow
blocks.zh:
sequenceDiagram
    participant S as 站点（手机）
    participant A as AP
    Note over S,A: 1. 发现
    A-->>S: 信标帧（被动扫描）
    S->>A: 探测请求（主动扫描）
    A-->>S: 探测响应
    Note over S,A: 2. 认证
    S->>A: 认证帧
    A-->>S: 认证帧（成功）
    Note over S,A: 3. 关联
    S->>A: 关联请求
    A-->>S: 关联响应（成功）
    Note over S,A: 现在数据帧可以流动了

blocks.en: Step 1, discovery, is how a station finds out what networks are nearby. It can scan passively — just listen on each channel for Beacon frames the AP broadcasts periodically — or actively, by sending a Probe Request and waiting for the AP's Probe Response. Either way the station learns the network's name (SSID), its identifier (BSSID), and its capabilities. Step 2, authentication, is a preliminary handshake that moves the relationship past 'total stranger.' Step 3, association, is where the station formally registers with this specific AP; only after a successful association can it become a full member and exchange ordinary data.
blocks.zh: 第一步“发现”，是站点弄清楚周围有哪些网络。它可以被动扫描——只在每个信道上聆听 AP 周期性广播的信标帧（Beacon）；也可以主动扫描——发出探测请求（Probe Request）并等待 AP 的探测响应（Probe Response）。无论哪种方式，站点都能获知网络名称（SSID）、标识符（BSSID）以及它的能力集。第二步“认证”，是一次初步握手，把双方的关系从“完全陌生”向前推进一步。第三步“关联”，是站点正式向这个特定 AP 登记；只有关联成功后，它才能成为正式成员并收发普通数据。

blocks.en: Why the order matters: the standard tracks the link with a state variable. A station starts unauthenticated and unassociated, may then become authenticated, and only after a successful association reaches the associated state where it is allowed to send the data frames that carry your traffic.
blocks.zh: 顺序为何重要：标准用一个状态变量来跟踪这条链路。站点起初既未认证也未关联，随后可能变为已认证，只有在关联成功后才进入“已关联”状态——此时它才被允许发送承载你流量的数据帧。

summary.en: Frame classes and the STA state machine

blocks.en: The standard keeps a per-link state variable inside the MLME with four main values: State 1 (unauthenticated, unassociated), State 2 (authenticated, unassociated), State 3 (associated, RSNA pending), and State 4 (associated, RSNA established or not required). Which value you are in gates which frames you may send. Frames are grouped into classes: Class 1 frames (Beacon, Probe, Authentication, control frames) are allowed even in State 1; Class 2 frames (Association/Reassociation/Disassociation) need at least State 2; and Class 3 frames — the Data frames that actually carry your traffic in an infrastructure BSS — require State 3 or State 4. That is precisely why discovery and authentication must come before association, and association before data: each step unlocks the next class of frame.
blocks.zh: 标准在 MLME 内部为每条链路保存一个状态变量，主要有四个取值：状态 1（未认证、未关联）、状态 2（已认证、未关联）、状态 3（已关联、RSNA 待定）、状态 4（已关联、RSNA 已建立或不需要）。你处于哪个状态，决定了你能发送哪些帧。帧被划分为若干类别：第 1 类帧（信标、探测、认证及控制帧）即使在状态 1 也允许发送；第 2 类帧（关联／重关联／解除关联）至少需要状态 2；而第 3 类帧——在基础设施 BSS 中真正承载流量的数据帧——则需要状态 3 或状态 4。这正是为什么发现与认证必须先于关联、关联必须先于数据：每一步都会解锁下一类帧。

cites.note: Components of the 802.11 architecture: BSS as basic building block; BSS membership is dynamic; joining via the synchronization procedure; becoming associated to access services
Connectivity-related services: a STA must be associated before the distribution system service can handle its MSDUs
Acquiring synchronization / scanning: passive scan (Beacon) vs active scan (Probe Request/Response)
STA authentication and association: state variables (States 1-4), state transition diagram, and frame classes 1/2/3 gated by state
